data.title: MarketAxess reports trading volume statistics for November 2024
data.description:
MarketAxess (MKTX) announced total average daily volume (ADV) of $44.9B for November, up 56% Y/Y and +3% compared to October 2024, driven by a rebound in U.S. Treasury ADV.

Total rates ADV reached $30.7 billion, showing a 112% increase from the previous year.

Total credit ADV of $14.3 billion was in line with the prior year, but decreased 5% compared to October 2024.

The preliminary FPM for total credit for November 2024 was approximately $146, down from $157 in the prior year, and down from $154 in October 2024.

Preliminary total credit FPM quarter-to-date through November 30 was approximately $151 compared to $149 in third quarter 2024.

The preliminary FPM for total rates for November 2024 was approximately $4.07, down from $5.07 in the prior year, and down from $4.40 in October 2024.

Notable achievements include record emerging markets ADV of $3.8 billion (up 15% Y/Y) and record municipal bond ADV of $631 million (up 5% Y/Y).

The company's portfolio trading solution showed positive engagement with 21 dealers and 26 long-only clients, with average trade size over $4 million notional.

A record 76% of portfolio trading volume was executed over X-Pro.

Shares of MarketAxess declined 0.64% in premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Daktronics beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Daktronics press release (NASDAQ:DAKT): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.22 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $208.33M (+4.5% Y/Y) beats by $5.74M.
  • Record cash flows from operations of $43.3 million for the fiscal second quarter and $62.8 million for the first six months of fiscal 2025.
  • Product order backlog of $236.0 million at October 26, 2024, compared to $267.2 million at the end of the first quarter of fiscal 2025 and $306.9 million at the end of the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
  • Product and service orders of $177.6 million for the quarter, a decrease of 3.3 percent from the second quarter of 2024 and $353.8 million on a year-to-date basis, a year-to-date increase of 3.3 percent.
  • Outlook: Daktronics enters the third quarter of fiscal 2025 with order growth of 3.3 percent year-over-year, and continues to expect order volume to increase for the full fiscal year. The first half of the fiscal year is typically the highest-volume sales of the year, and are expected to be at normal "seasonal" levels for the remainder of fiscal 2025.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Chewy raises FY2024 outlook after mixed FQ3 results
Eli Lilly’s Zepbound outperforms Novo's Wegovy for weight loss in head-to-head trial
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) said Wednesday its weight-loss drug Zepbound (tirzepatide) provided a 47% greater relative weight loss compared to Danish rival Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) in the first head-to-head trial of the two blockbuster weight loss drugs.

In a study sponsored by Lilly, people taking Zepbound lost an average of 20% of their body weight, while those on Wegovy shed 14%. Specifically, participants using Zepbound lost 50.3 lbs (22.8 kg) and participants on Wegovy lost 33.1 lbs (15.0 kg).

At 72 weeks, Zepbound beat Wegovy on both the primary endpoint and all five key secondary endpoints in this 751-person trial (SURMOUNT-5 phase 3b) of adults living with obesity or overweight with at least one weight-related medical problem and without diabetes.

In a key secondary endpoint, 31.6% of people taking Zepbound achieved at least 25% body weight loss compared to 16.1% of those taking Wegovy.

The full results will be published in a peer-reviewed journal and presented at a medical meeting next year, Lilly said.

Tirzepatide is commercialized for adults with obesity or with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems as Zepbound in the U.S. and Mounjaro in some global markets outside the U.S. The drug is also commercialized as Mounjaro for adults with type 2 diabetes in the U.S.

Semaglutide is commercialized as Wegovy for people living with obesity or for adults with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems and Ozempic for people with type 2 diabetes.

U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) dropped marginally premarket. Lilly (LLY) shares rose as much as 2% in trading before U.S. markets opened.

data.title: Southland's unit gets $60M wastewater treatment plant contract
data.description:
• Southland (NYSE:SLND) said its unit has been awarded a $60 million contract for the construction of a new wastewater treatment plant in the Southwest.
  • The range of work includes construction of new headworks, influent pump station, extended aeration basin, administration building, utility water station, and renovation of the crew building.
  • The firm said the award will be included in fourth quarter 2024 backlog.

data.title: Air Industries gets $4M contract
data.description:
• Air Industries (NYSE:AIRI) said it has received a follow-on contract valued at ~$4 million for arresting gear components for the F-35 Lightning II fighter aircraft.
  • The firm said the production of the arresting gear components is expected to begin in the first half of 2025.
  • The contract is slated for completion by year end 2025.

data.title: Activist investor renews campaign for Rio Tinto to quit London listing
data.description:
Activist investor Palliser Capital said Wednesday it wants Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) to scrap its primary London listing and unify its corporate structure in Australia.

In a letter to Rio's (RIO) board, the U.K.-based hedge fund said the miner's dual-listed structure "has proved to be an unmitigated failure for shareholders and requires urgent unification into a single Australian-domiciled holding company," adding shareholders have lost an estimated $50B in value due to the dual structure.

Rio (RIO) CEO Jakob Stausholm said in July that the company would not unify as an Australian company following a previous call by Palliser to do so; he said Rio's own review concluded changing the structure would destroy value for shareholders.

Palliser rejects that view: "Based on our extensive analysis, we believe that this readily actionable step would resolve the value destructive inefficiencies of the outdated DLC structure and present a path to $28B (27%) upside in the near term for PLC shareholders and further upside for the combined group over the medium term."

data.title: Dollarama reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Dollarama press release (OTCPK:DLMAF): Q3 sales increased by 5.7% to C$1,562.6 million, compared to C$1,477.7 million
  • This increase was driven by growth in the total number of stores over the past 12 months (from 1,541 stores on October 29, 2023, to 1,601 stores on October 27, 2024) and increased comparable store sales.
  • Comparable store sales increased by 3.3%, over and above 11.1% growth in the corresponding period of the previous year
  • EPS of C$0.98.
  • 18 net new stores opened, compared to 16 net new stores

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Foot Locker trims FY sales, profit outlook after Q3 miss
Chewy Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 misses by $0.03, revenue of $2.88B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Chewy press release (NYSE:CHWY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $2.88B (+4.8% Y/Y)  beats by $20M.
